summary: Mission Control is currently a fully mock-driven UI. All data originates from sample.json / state.json with no live agent system integration.

findings.item: All agents, tasks, projects, memories, docs, calendar events, activities, office layout, suggested tools
findings.reason: sample.json is the source-of-truth fallback data loaded by lib/store.tsx. Every entity in it is fabricated.

findings.item: Identical copy of sample.json data (with minor model field differences)
findings.reason: state.json was seeded from sample.json and never updated by a real agent system. All content is placeholder.
Hardcoded status string, not computed from actual agent activity.
Placeholder mission statement from March 2025. Not sourced from any config file.

findings.item: 5 agents: Claw Prime, Scout, Forge, Relay, Sentinel
findings.reason: Agent names, roles, specialties, models, and statuses are all fabricated. No connection to actual OpenClaw agent registry.
All tasks are placeholder items. Assignee IDs, priorities, due dates, tags are invented. Not connected to any task queue.

findings.item: 3 projects: Mission Control UI, Operations & Safety, Content & Comms
findings.reason: Project cards with milestones, progress %, and health indicators are all dummy data.
Memory entries are fictional. Not pulled from actual OpenClaw memory files (MEMORY.md, memory/*.md).

findings.item: 6 documents: doc-arch, doc-prd-mc, doc-research-privacy, doc-planning-ops, doc-draft-comms, doc-content-blog
findings.reason: Documentation entries are placeholder. Not sourced from actual workspace .md files.
Calendar events are hardcoded. Not connected to actual OpenClaw cron jobs.
Activity entries are fake strings. Not connected to real tool call logs or agent action history.
Zone coordinates and agent positions are hardcoded pixel values. Not derived from any real layout system.
Tool suggestions are placeholder ideas, not AI-generated or real backlog items.

findings.item: cloneSample() fallback and POST initial state
findings.reason: Store initializes from sample.json when no state.json exists. This is the bootstrap mechanism but means all data originates from mock source.
Agent model input has hardcoded default value. Not persisted or connected to actual config.
Auto-refresh interval is hardcoded at 30 seconds. Not persisted.

findings.item: Background gradient SVG and Meeting Zone styling
findings.reason: These are UI design elements, not data. However, Meeting Zone highlight logic is overly simplistic (any active agent = highlight).

findings.item: recentTasks filter uses t.assignee === 'me' || t.assignee === user.id
findings.reason: Uses legacy 'assignee' field that doesn't exist on the Task type (should be assigneeId). This filter likely returns nothing.

areasSummary.Office screen agents: ALL MOCKED — fabricated agent names, statuses, positions
areasSummary.Activity feed: ALL MOCKED — fake strings, no real tool call logging
areasSummary.Task Board: ALL MOCKED — placeholder tasks not from any queue
areasSummary.Calendar: ALL MOCKED — hardcoded events not from real cron jobs
areasSummary.Projects: ALL MOCKED — dummy project cards with invented progress
areasSummary.Memories: ALL MOCKED — fictional entries, not from actual memory files
areasSummary.Docs: ALL MOCKED — placeholder documents, not from workspace files
areasSummary.Dashboard stats: PARTIALLY REAL — computed from mock data (activeTasks, scheduledToday, agentsActive derive from state.json which is itself mocked)
